questions.question: What is the topic for Week 33?
options: B. POINTERS OF A HEALTHY RELATIONSHIP
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-Three is DEFINE YOUR RELATIONSHIP.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 32?
questions.explanation: Live Healthy was the topic for Week 32.

questions.question: Can two walk together, unless they are ________?
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Amos 3:3) asks 'Can two walk together, unless they are agreed?'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Amos 3:3.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 33 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Can two walk together, unless they are agreed?
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Amos 3:3.

questions.question: According to the introduction, a normal intentional premarital relationship must have marriage as a destination.
questions.explanation: The text states 'A normal and correct intentional premarital relationship/courtship... must have marriage as a destination.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, why are there lots of breakups in premarital relationships?
options: A. Because people don't have enough money
B. Because many of the relationships were accidental
C. Because people are too young
D. Because they listen to bad advice
questions.explanation: The text says 'There are lots of breakups in premarital relationships because many of the relationships were accidental.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'POINTERS OF A HEALTHY RELATIONSHIP'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, if someone shows care, like visiting you or supporting you financially, does it automatically mean you are in a courtship?
options: B. Only if they are a believer
C. No, it does not mean you are in a courtship
D. Yes, if they give you money
questions.explanation: The text states 'Just because someone shows care, like visiting you or supporting you financially, does not mean you are in a courtship (Proverbs 23:7).'

questions.question: What should you do if you are unsure of someone's intentions in a relationship?
options: B. Assume they want to marry you
questions.explanation: The text advises: 'Always ask questions if you are unsure of someone’s intentions.'

questions.question: What does Outline I say about assuming you are engaged?
options: A. Assume you are engaged if they buy you a ring
B. Assume you are engaged if you hold hands
C. Never assume you are engaged unless there has been a formal proposal and mutual consent
D. Assume you are engaged if you go to the same church
questions.explanation: Point 3 states 'Never assume you are engaged unless there has been a formal proposal and mutual consent (Genesis 24:48-51, 57-58).'

questions.question: What is described as a 'key sign' of a well-defined relationship where there is no fear of the unknown?
questions.explanation: Point 6 says 'Peace: Peace is a key sign of a well-defined relationship. There should be no fear of the unknown or unnecessary suspense.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding accountability and open communication in a relationship?
questions.explanation: Point 5 on Accountability cites '(Proverbs 10:9)'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'BE INTENTIONAL'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what does it mean to be deliberate in a relationship?
options: A. To keep your options open
B. To set clear timelines and goals
C. To wait for the other person to decide everything
D. To date for at least five years
questions.explanation: The text says 'To be deliberate in a relationship is to set clear timelines and goals...'

questions.question: According to the lesson, a well-defined relationship must have a take-off point and a landing point.
questions.explanation: The text states 'A well-defined relationship must have a take-off point and a landing point (Genesis 24:51).'

questions.question: What does the lesson say the would-be couples must abstain from since they are not legally married?
options: C. Premarital sex and other sexual vices
questions.explanation: The text states 'Since the two parties are not legally married, they must abstain from premarital sex and other sexual vices (Hebrews 13:4).'

questions.question: Which biblical figure is given as an example of standing firm and persevering until he married?
questions.explanation: The text says 'A clear-cut relationship stands firm even when challenges arise just as Jacob persevered until he married Rachel (Genesis 29:20-28; Proverbs 12:3).'

questions.question: The conclusion advises you not to leave your relationship to chance, but to define it and have a direction.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states 'Do not leave your relationship to chance, define it and have a direction for where you are heading.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Now Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.'
questions.verse_text: Now Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
questions.explanation: This is verse 16 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Genesis 29:15), what did Laban ask Jacob regarding his service?
options: C. 'Why did you leave your father?'
questions.explanation: Verse 15 says 'Because you are my relative, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what should your wages be?'

questions.question: In Genesis 29:17, how are Leah and Rachel described?
options: B. Leah had delicate eyes, but Rachel was beautiful of form and appearance
C. Leah was tall, Rachel was short
D. Leah was strong, Rachel was weak
questions.explanation: Verse 17 says 'Leah’s eyes were delicate, but Rachel was beautiful of form and appearance.'

questions.question: According to Genesis 29:18, what did Jacob offer to do to marry Rachel?
options: A. Pay Laban 100 shekels of silver
B. Serve Laban for seven years
C. Give Laban ten flocks of sheep
questions.explanation: Verse 18 says 'I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter.'

questions.question: According to Genesis 29:20, how did the seven years seem to Jacob?
options: B. Like only a few days
questions.explanation: Verse 20 says 'and they seemed only a few days to him because of the love he had for her.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding not wandering aimlessly and ensuring a relationship has a clear path with well-defined stages?
questions.explanation: Point 4 (Direction) cites '(Proverbs 29:18a; 1 Corinthians 14:40)'.

questions.question: The relationship must be established and driven by a good and realistic ________.
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'The relationship must be established and driven by a good and realistic vision (Proverbs 29:18).'

questions.question: Shared interests or working together automatically equates to compatibility for marriage.
questions.explanation: Outline I explicitly states 'Shared interests or working together does not automatically equate to compatibility for marriage.'

questions.question: Truthfulness must be intentionally exhibited by the two parties in the relationship.
questions.explanation: Outline II concludes 'Truthfulness must be intentionally exhibited by the two parties in the relationship.'

questions.question: What must be set during courtship to guide the would-be couples on the 'dos and don'ts' of the relationship?
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Necessary boundaries must be set during the courtship to guide the would-be couples on the ‘dos and don’ts’ of the relationship.'

questions.question: A formal proposal does not need to be extravagant; what matters is that it is:
options: B. Clear and agreed upon by both parties
C. Done in front of the whole church
questions.explanation: Point 3 says 'The proposal does not need to be extravagant; what matters is that it is clear and agreed upon by both parties.'
